q: Antibiotic resistance evolves in bacterial populations through which mechanism?
options: Bacteria sense the antibiotic and deliberately mutate the targeted gene to become resistant
Antibiotics cause bacteria to produce protective enzymes by activating latent resistance genes
Random mutations that confer resistance pre-exist in the population; antibiotic use kills sensitive bacteria, selecting for resistant variants that reproduce and dominate
Bacteria absorb antibiotic molecules and convert them into nutrients, gaining energy to outcompete sensitive strains
explanation: Resistance arises from pre-existing random mutations; antibiotics act as a selective pressure, eliminating susceptible bacteria and allowing resistant mutants to proliferate — natural selection in action.
